summary: Provision an Amazon Pinpoint project (application) as the container for a messaging campaign — email, SMS, or push. Creating the project is an engineer-gated form; the messaging channels that actually send are always finished by the engineer afterward.

operations.title: Create a new Pinpoint project
operations.summary: Request a new Pinpoint project for messaging channels and campaigns. An engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it's applied.
operations.description: Specify a new aws_pinpoint_app: just its name. Messaging channels (email, SMS, push credentials), sending limits, and campaigns are always finished by the engineer — this form provisions the empty project container. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: app_name
params.label: Project name
params.help: The project's name in the Pinpoint console.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Project name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this project is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this project.

decisions: Messaging channels (email, SMS, push notification credentials) are separate resources added as engineer follow-ups once this project exists
Sending limits, quiet hours, and the campaign hook (a Lambda pre-send filter) keep Pinpoint defaults until an engineer sets them
